title: potentilla kelloggii
intro: Potentilla kelloggii, also known as Kellogg's cinquefoil, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the western United States. It belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is recognized by its palmate leaves and white or pink flowers. Potentilla kelloggii has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its health benefits are said to include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing.
